name: Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón – circular by the Camino del Val del Asón (PR-S 14)
name_original: Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón – circular por el Camino del Val del Asón (PR-S 14)
region_intro: Hiking around Arredondo, a municipality in Cantabria, Spain, offers diverse outdoor experiences within a striking natural landscape. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, verdant valleys, and significant geological formations, notably within the Collados del Asón Natural Park. This area features limestone outcrops, ancient beech woods, and unique karstic features shaped by glacial action, such as the Circo de Hondojón. The Valle del Asón, with the meandering Asón River, further defines the lush environment for exploration. Best hiking trails around Arredondo The most popular hiking route is Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón – circular by the Camino del Val del Asón (PR-S 14), a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park's diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Porracolina via Calseca Stream Trail, a difficult 7.6 miles (12.3 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain and leads to Pico Porracolina, offering expansive views. Local hikers also love the Asón Waterfall loop from Asón, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail leading through the Valle del Asón to the impressive Asón Waterfall, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Arredondo is defined by majestic mountains, verdant valleys, and dramatic waterfalls. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Arredondo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Arredondo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Arredondo?
A: Arredondo offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 90 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to different fitness levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Arredondo?
A: The terrain in Arredondo is incredibly diverse, characterized by majestic mountains, verdant valleys, and significant geological formations within the Collados del Asón Natural Park. You'll encounter limestone outcrops, ancient beech woods, and unique karstic features like the spectacular Circo de Hondojón. Paths can range from well-trodden trails to steeper, potentially muddy sections, especially in areas with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Arredondo?
A: Arredondo's special climatology makes it suitable for hiking through much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer is also popular, though some higher altitude routes might be more exposed. Winter can be beautiful, but some paths may require more caution due to potential snow or ice, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Arredondo?
A: Yes, Arredondo features several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996716522/parque-natural-de-los-collados-del-ason-circular-by-the-camino-del-val-del-ason-pr-s-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón – circular by the Camino del Val del Asón (PR-S 14)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.8 km trail exploring the natural park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10506627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall loop from Asón&lt;/a&gt;, which is a shorter 7.7 km moderate route.

Q: Are there any impressive waterfalls to see while hiking in Arredondo?
A: Absolutely! The region is famous for its dramatic waterfalls. The most iconic is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which marks the origin of the Asón River and plunges over 70 meters. You can also discover other beautiful cascades like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4035715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Garma Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5283013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suscuaja River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks I can visit on a hike?
A: Arredondo offers numerous stunning viewpoints and landmarks. You can hike to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3817978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Vizmaya&lt;/a&gt; for extensive views of the surrounding towns, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4118907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; for a spectacular perspective of the cascade. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2092775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Alisas&lt;/a&gt; also provides panoramic vistas.

Q: Can I explore any caves or unique geological formations in the area?
A: Yes, Arredondo is a significant area for speleology. The region is known for its extensive karstic features and a vast underground network of caves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Coventosa&lt;/a&gt; is a notable attraction, forming part of a complex system with thousands of cataloged caves, some reaching great depths and containing underground rivers.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Arredondo?
A: Yes, Arredondo provides several easy and moderate trails suitable for families. While some routes require good fitness, there are 22 easy routes available on komoot, offering gentler paths through the valleys and natural parks that are enjoyable for all ages. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Arredondo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Arredondo, especially within the natural park areas. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Please be mindful of local regulations and ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb the natural environment or other hikers.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Arredondo?
A: The hiking experiences in Arredondo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the majestic mountains, verdant valleys, and dramatic waterfalls. The diverse terrain, from ancient beech woods to unique karstic landscapes, is often highlighted as a major draw.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Arredondo offers several difficult routes. One such demanding trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996940056/pico-porracolina-via-calseca-stream-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Porracolina via Calseca Stream Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.3 km path with significant elevation gain leading to the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Porracolina&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views. There are 29 difficult routes in total.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Arredondo?
A: While specific parking information varies by trailhead, many popular starting points for hikes in Arredondo, especially those within the Collados del Asón Natural Park or near major attractions like the Asón Waterfall, typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the route descriptions on komoot or local signage for the most accurate parking details for your chosen trail.
tips:
The Cubera cave is located at kilometer 2.8 of the road that goes from Arredondo to La Sia and its mouth is at the same level as the road and it has a spring that drains its waters from the currents of the system that predominates in the cave. zone.

This cavity is divided into three zones according to its characteristics. Thus we have:

The Fossil Network of the Sima de Cueto which is characterized by having huge corridors in which there is no water around it and the ground is made up of large stone blocks which makes it difficult to transit through this area.

Another section is the Intermediate Network which is made up of a large number of caves and labyrinth-like paths, these suppose an excavation after the waters and has several levels, these connect the Sima del Cueto and La Coventosa.

The third section in the Coventosa Gallery, which is located next to the previously described labyrinthine area, communicates with almost all the galleries as well as with the Cubera spring.

Information taken from: https://www.viajarporcantabria.com/la-cueva-de-cubera/

Wonderful place to rest at its entrance if you have passed near a route (a very cool and sheltered place) or to enter and marvel at the wonderful rooms inside. Specifically, I know the Hall of Ghosts and I recommend it (yes, with a person who knows by your side because it is not to enter without a minimum knowledge, it can be very dangerous)
Very quiet place next to the river to have a good time. It can be reached either by car or on foot.
